messages.content: Wow, you truly made my day:$  Thank you so much, and wishing you enjoy the rest of the day

Hi there,

This is Hazel from the Chatty Team.

I’m following up to let you know that our tech team has customized the chat button position as per your request. We’ve adjusted it using CSS to ensure it does not overlap with the “Back to Top” icon on both desktop and mobile versions.
[https://capture.avada.io/i/lqlpmubQiBLn](https://capture.avada.io/i/lqlpmubQiBLn)

Currently, you can enable the Order tracking option on the widget to allow customers to track order status. However, it is currently supported only for orders placed on the Shopify platform, and I am afraid it is not possible to add a custom block to track orders on Amazon.

What email should I use there? Because I normally log in from Shopify.

Oh, let me check it further

Your Chatty account is already activated and working fine. The issue is only with logging in directly via the web


Since you normally open Chatty from Shopify admin, your account was set up through Shopify authentication and doesn't have a separate password yet.


To log in directly on the web, please:

1. Go to app.chatty.net (http://app.chatty.net/)
2. Click Forgot password
3. Enter your Shopify store owner email
4. Check your inbox for the reset link → set a new password


After that you'll be able to log in from both Shopify admin and the web directly."

Can you check if your device is in Silent mode, Do Not Disturb mode, or any similar setting that may block notifications?
And please make sure you already enabled "Chatty notifications" ( [Banners, Sounds, Badges](https://prnt.sc/wnXCSq0UUQrB)) in your device’s settings under "Settings > Notifications"
Yes, everything has been set to be able to receive notifications and sounds, etc. But to be sure, your app does send notifications in the status bar and sound when applicable?
Yes 😊 chatty sends notifications. In this case, I’d suggest reinstalling the mobile app (remove and add it again) to see if that helps resolve the issue.
Can it also not detect whether someone is logged in or not? Because the dealers are the people that are logged in and the customers are the people that aren't logged in

Ah we can detect the customer login status, but it will help the system to show their name (for the personalization purpose) or not require them to provide too much information again in the chat only.

In the meantime, I just updated an instruction to guide the AI not to show the product price in the response.

Chatty doesn't display or store your Shopify API key — we connect to your store through Shopify's secure OAuth, so there's no API key shown in our platform.



To find your Shopify API key, please go to your Shopify Admin → Settings → Apps and sales channels → Develop apps. Your API credentials are managed there.



If you need more help, Shopify has a guide here: https://help.shopify.com/en/manual/apps/app-types/custom-apps

Sad, Shopify closed Custom app since Jan 1, 2026 and it has been really hard for us.

We will get back to you as soon as possible with further details on the request.

We highly appreciate your kind patience in the meantime.

Hi again,

Regarding the Scroll (Back-to-top) button appearing higher on the product page, our team has investigated and found that this is actually caused by the button’s CSS (https://capture.avada.io/i/cYjLQIRKsjWd). It is currently adding the height of the Add-to-cart section to its bottom spacing, which is why it’s being pushed upward.

We would like to share that this behavior is not related to the previous adjustments made by our team. However, we’ve added a custom CSS fix directly in the app to correct the positioning, so the button should display properly without being pushed up. (https://capture.avada.io/i/MmIUZGJfahZV)

Please help us reload the page and double-check the results on your end (https://capture.avada.io/i/t8YpDBzftT5x, https://capture.avada.io/i/VkMg08R9mp4B).

We look forward to hearing your feedback.

Regards,
Jade

Mình sẽ nhắn lại bạn sớm

Mình đã kiểm tra rồi ạ. Lỗi "You have requested a verification code too many times" này là do phía Meta (Facebook) giới hạn số lần gửi mã xác minh, không phải lỗi từ Chatty.



Khi bạn kết nối Instagram/Facebook vào Chatty, Chatty sẽ chuyển bạn sang trang đăng nhập của Facebook để xác minh. Phần nhập mã xác minh SĐT này hoàn toàn do Meta quản lý - Chatty không can thiệp được vào bước này.


Cách xử lý:

1. Chờ khoảng 24 tiếng rồi thử lại - Meta sẽ tự gỡ giới hạn sau một thời gian
2. Khi thử lại, bạn có thể thử đổi trình duyệt khác hoặc xoá cookies trước khi kết nối
3. Nếu sau 24 tiếng vẫn bị, bạn có thể liên hệ Meta Support để được hỗ trợ trực tiếp


Mình hiểu là bất tiện cho bạn, nhưng vì đây là bảo mật của Facebook nên phía Chatty không có cách nào bỏ qua giới hạn này được ạ. Bạn thử lại sau 24 tiếng giúp mình nha

tôi đang có 2 phương án để add whatsapp vào chatty, 1 là tạo tài khoản whatsapp doanh nghiệp trong BM meta rồi link BM đó vào chatty, 2 là tạo mới trực tiếp trên chatty. Bạn thấy phương án nào là tốt nhất

Dạ bạn, mình recommend tạo trực tiếp trên Chatty luôn, đây là cách nhanh nhất:



1. Vào Chatty → Channels → WhatsApp → Manage
2. Bấm "Connect to WhatsApp"
3. Popup Facebook sẽ mở ra — bạn đăng nhập Facebook, rồi có thể tạo mới WhatsApp Business Account ngay trong đó luôn
4. Chọn hoặc thêm số điện thoại → xác minh → done!


Nếu bạn đã có sẵn WhatsApp Business Account trong Meta Business Manager thì trong bước 3, bạn chỉ cần chọn account có sẵn thay vì tạo mới. Không cần setup gì trước ở Meta BM, Chatty sẽ hướng dẫn bạn qua từng bước trong popup luôn ạ

First of all, thank you so much for your kind patience while our team reviewed your request. Upon checking, I am pleased to share that at the moment, we do support deeper integration with Chatty through our JavaScript SDK, which allows you to programmatically control the chatbox and tailor the customer experience directly on your website.

* Chat opened
* Chat closed
* Message sent
* Message received

* When you are online, the AI will automatically assign conversations to you.

* Conversations are set to only assign to member Kaley K when this member is online
* The option “Conversations stay with assigned member (or stay unassigned)” is enabled

To avoid this issue, we recommend updating the setting so it does not only assign to online members: https://capture.avada.io/i/nWh3y6iwjsVl

This way, conversations can still be assigned to a member even when they are offline, and you will receive the email notification.

como puedo llegar a configurar el horario de chatty para mis clientes?
Hola, ¿puedo saber si quieres configurar un recordatorio en Chatty?
¿Podrías compartir más detalles sobre tu idea para que podamos ayudarte mejor?

Hola de nuevo,

Estoy aquí para ponerte al día sobre el problema en marcha. Para asegurarme de que tu IA ofrece las respuestas más precisas, quería compartir un vistazo rápido a cómo prioriza la información. Actualmente, la IA sigue esta jerarquía:
1. Comportamientos predeterminados de la app (por ejemplo, escenarios postventa)
2. Escenarios personalizados
3. PREGUNTAS FRECUENTES / PREGUNTAS Y RESPUESTAS (Alta Prioridad)
4. Fuentes de datos (URLs, archivos, datos de producto)

Dado que la IA prioriza la sección de preguntas frecuentes sobre las fuentes generales de datos, ignorará la información actualizada en tu sitio web si las preguntas frecuentes contienen datos antiguos o contradictorios.

Para obtener los mejores resultados, por favor asegúrate de que tus preguntas frecuentes sean coherentes con tus últimas URLs. Una vez que estas se alineen, la IA dejará de dar respuestas contradictorias.

¡Espero que esta información te ayude! Si tienes alguna duda, ¡por favor háznoslo saber!

Un cordial saludo,
Phoebe | Equipo de soporte de Avada

About Us
 

About Us

At BoostBox, we are committed to enhancing the overall health and vitality of our customers through a carefully curated selection of wellness and beauty products. Founded in 2026 by a group of university students from Toronto Metropolitan University. Our team comprises industry experts who meticulously research and select each product, ensuring that they meet the highest standards of quality and efficacy.

Our Mission

Our mission is to provide unique solutions that cater to the diverse needs of individuals seeking holistic well-being. We understand that wellness is not a one-size-fits-all approach; therefore, our offerings, including the popular "Beauty Support Box" and "Digestive Support Box," are designed to address specific health concerns and promote a balanced lifestyle. We believe that informed choices lead to better health outcomes, which is why we provide detailed information about the ingredients and benefits of our products. Studies indicate that 70% of consumers are more likely to purchase products that are backed by scientific research, and we strive to align with this expectation.

What Makes Us Different? 

What sets BoostBox apart is our unwavering dedication to customer satisfaction and education. We not only offer products but also resources and guidance to empower our customers in their wellness journeys. By fostering a community centered around health and beauty, we aim to inspire individuals to prioritize their well-being and embrace a lifestyle that supports their goals. Together, we can achieve a healthier, more vibrant life.

As I checked, this behavior is related to a current limitation of the AI. While the AI is able to read and analyze images, its primary capability at the moment is focused on product recognition and recommendations. It is not yet able to reliably analyze order-related issues based on images.

Because of this, when the customer shared an image, the AI likely shifted its context toward product suggestions, which may also have caused the unexpected language switch and loss of the original flow.

As a workaround, we recommend training the AI to better handle these scenarios. For example, by guiding it to suggest contacting human support or forwarding the query when a customer raises an order-related issue. This can help avoid situations where the AI is unable to provide accurate assistance.

Yes, this can definitely be adjusted. 

You can configure the AI to recommend products less frequently, or only when customers explicitly ask for them. This helps ensure the suggestions are more relevant and not intrusive. 

To achieve this, you can update the AI behavior in: AI Assistant => Instructions => Behaviors. You can add a rule such as: “Only recommend products when the customer clearly expresses a need or asks for suggestions. Avoid proactive product recommendations.” This way, the AI will focus more on answering questions instead of pushing products unnecessarily.

Hi Chatty Support,

We're using Chatty (by Avada) on our Shopify store and have a specific use case we'd like to implement.

In our FAQ content, we have multiple references to "contact us via this chat" — for example in answers about order tracking, returns, and account issues. These are currently plain text.

We'd like to turn these references into clickable links or buttons that:
1. Open the Chatty widget directly (ideally with the chat already open, not just the launcher visible)
2. Optionally pre-fill or trigger a specific conversation flow or topic

Our questions:
- Is there a JavaScript method or public API we can call to open the Chatty widget programmatically? (e.g. something like ChattyWidget.open() or a window.postMessage event)
- Is there a URL hash or parameter (e.g. ?chat=open) that triggers the widget to open on page load?
- Can we pass a pre-filled message or route the user to a specific bot flow via the trigger?
- Is there official documentation covering widget trigger methods for developers?

We're comfortable implementing a custom JS snippet or an HTML anchor tag — we just need to know the correct method to trigger the widget from our metaobject-rendered Liquid content.

Thank you,
Olfazeta by Chogan
Hi there! I'm Linda, thanks for reaching out. Let me assist you further
Hello thanks
Thanks for sharing. Let me check on it.

Hi there, Thank you for reaching out! 1. Opening the Chatty widget programmatically: We currently support deeplinks using URL hash fragments. To open the chat widget directly, you can use: #chatty-chat In your FAQ content, simply replace your "contact us via this chat" text with a clickable link like this: contact us via this chat When clicked, this will open the Chatty widget and take the customer straight to the chat screen. 2. Opening the widget on page load via URL: Yes! You can append the hash to any page URL, for example: https://yourstore.com/pages/faq#chatty-chat The widget will automatically open to the chat screen when the page loads with this URL. 3. Other available deeplinks: We also support these sections: • #chatty-home — Opens the widget home screen • #chatty-tracking — Opens order tracking • #chatty-help — Opens the help/FAQ section 4. Pre-filled messages or specific bot flows: This is not currently supported, but it's a great suggestion! I've forwarded this to our product team for future consideration. 5. Documentation: You can find the deeplink settings in the app under Chatbox > Advanced > Deeplinks, where you can also copy each link directly. Since your FAQ content is rendered via metaobject Liquid templates, the HTML anchor approach () should work perfectly within your content blocks. Let me know if you need any further assistance with the setup! Best regard, Linda

I've looked into it and here's what's happening:



The error message: "(#100) The action is invalid as it's not the thread owner" This is an Instagram API restriction, not a Chatty bug. Here's why it occurs:


Instagram uses something called the Handover Protocol - it means only one app can "own" and reply to a conversation at a time. When another app (or Instagram's own inbox) takes over a conversation, other apps lose the ability to send messages in that thread.




This specific conversation's ownership was taken by another app or by Instagram/Meta Business Suite. Your other conversations are still owned by Chatty, which is why they work fine.


Common causes:

• You or a team member replied to this conversation directly from Instagram app or Meta Business Suite
• Another third-party app connected to your Instagram account took over the thread


How to fix it:

1. Check if you're using any other apps connected to your Instagram DM (e.g. other live chat tools, CRM, or Meta Business Suite inbox)
2. If yes, try disconnecting the other app, or avoid replying from Instagram/Meta Business Suite directly for conversations you want to manage through Chatty
3. For this specific conversation: When the customer sends a new message, thread ownership will reset and Chatty should be able to reply again


To prevent this in the future:

• Always reply through Chatty for conversations you want Chatty to manage
• Avoid replying to the same conversation from multiple platforms
Oh, so we can reply to a customer’s message on Instagram, but if the customer restarts the conversation, then Chatty can respond again. Is that correct?

Almost. Let me clarify: You're right that if a message was replied to from Instagram directly (or another app), Chatty temporarily loses the ability to respond in that thread.



However, the customer doesn't need to restart the conversation. They just need to send a new message in the same conversation, once they do, the thread ownership resets and Chatty will be able to reply again.

Ah yeah, I see let's me explain more clearly for you.

"Enable" or "Activate" means turning something on so it’s active or functional. 
"Sync" means connecting and updating data between systems so they match. Example: “You need to sync your products” => you’re making sure the AI has the latest product info from your store.
In short: enable = turn it on, sync = update/align the data.

Ah, this will depend on the Markets settings you have set up in Shopify.

Please let me know

Ah, when a user clicks on this link, Shopify determines how it's displayed based on the visitor's market. And this is how they determine it:
* **IP & Geolocation:** It automatically detects where the customer is physically located.
* **Browser Language:** It checks the 'Accept-Language' settings in their browser.
* **Specific Domain:** If a customer goes directly to your `.fr` domain, the system will prioritize the France market automatically.
So basically, we don't manage these markets; it's Shopify's Markets mechanism and the customer's device that display the corresponding subdomain.

Hi Wickii team, 

This is Jade from the Avada Support Team, and I hope you are doing well. 

I am writing to follow up on your recent feedback on the AI response. Our team has checked the case and would like to share that at the moment, the AI’s follow-up question logic is designed to suggest adding to cart the products it has previously recommended as relevant. 

In this case, the AI is not aware that the metal stands are already included in the Wood Wicks 20 pack, so it treats them as separate items. The reason is that, while the product page does mention **“Each wick comes with a metal wick tab”**, this information is currently placed in a section that isn’t part of the main product description. As a result, it isn’t being synced to the app, so the AI doesn’t have access to that detail. (https://capture.avada.io/i/L398X55NUbKT)

As a temporary solution, we recommend adding this information directly into the product description or adding the product FAQs (https://capture.avada.io/i/44PblCzFf4zL). This will help the AI better understand the product and avoid making redundant suggestions like this. 

In parallel, our team is actively working on an improvement that will allow the AI to read more content displayed on the product page. This should help it better understand detailed product information, and we’re aiming to roll out this enhancement in the near future.

Thank you for your understanding and cooperation in this matter. If you have any other questions, please contact us for further support.

Regards,
Jade

Hi there,

I’d like to follow up regarding your request on customizing AI responses. Our tech team has checked further, and here are the updates:

* For the case where AI cannot find a product, we have customized the Instructions so that AI will now respond exactly as you requested. You can see the attached images for reference.
* Regarding the request to prevent AI from recommending products, this is currently not supported. The reason is that if we block AI from making recommendations, it will also block future cases when customers ask about products that are available in your store. Additionally, setting the instruction to stop responding when a product is not found would also prevent AI from searching and suggesting similar products (for example, those with a few matching words in the title).

Therefore, at this stage, AI can only respond as you requested in the scenario where it cannot find the product.

Please help me test this update and let me know if it works as expected.

Best regards,
Hazel
Avada Support Team

Hi there,

Thank you for sharing the two conversation examples. Please see our clarification below:



**Issue 1 – International Shipping FAQ Not Triggered**

In the customer’s original message, the question contains additional context beyond a general shipping inquiry. Instead of directly asking *“Do you ship internationally?”*, the message includes two clear intents:

* Whether you can ship a specific product to Canada
* If not, where they can purchase it and have it shipped to Canada

Because the question combines multiple intents and references a specific product and location, the AI interprets it as a case-specific inquiry rather than a general policy question. As a result, it does not match the FAQ entry the same way it would when the question is asked directly and clearly (e.g., “Do you ship internationally?”).

This multi-intent structure is the main reason the AI response differs from the predefined FAQ answer.



**Issue 2 – Incorrect Product Suggestion from Image Recognition**

In this case, the desired behavior (always adding a fallback sentence when suggesting a product based on an image or description) should be configured directly within your AI Instruction/Guideline settings.

You may consider refining the instruction with a rule such as:

> Whenever you suggest a product based on an image or user description, always add:
> “If this is not the correct product you're looking for, please contact [service@ubbiworld.com](mailto:service@ubbiworld.com) for further assistance.”

Since customers often write in a narrative or conversational style, the instruction may need to be adjusted to ensure it consistently applies to all image-recognition and product-suggestion scenarios.



We recommend reviewing and fine-tuning the Instruction section to ensure this requirement is clearly enforced.


Please let us know if you need any clarification.

En este caso, como los datos están en el lado Meta, no pudimos comprobar más por ti. Te sugiero seguir la lista de abajo para comprobar bien tu cuenta Meta y así resolver esta situación:

1. completar el registro final de la API:
Incluso si el número de teléfono se añade a la cuenta de WhatsApp Business (WABA), debe pasar por un "Registro" formal a través de la API para pasar de Pendiente a Conectado. Meta requiere un disparador específico de API para autorizar el número para automatización empresarial en lugar de uso personal.

2. Verificar el OTP de 6 dígitos (SMS/Voz):
El estado "Pendiente" suele significar que el código de verificación de 6 dígitos no se ha introducido con éxito. Si la OTP fue solicitada pero no recibida ni registrada, el proceso permanece atascado. _Nota: Los intentos fallidos frecuentes pueden llevar a un cierre patronal de 24 a 48 horas._

3. Desconectar de la aplicación estándar de WhatsApp:
Un número de teléfono no puede estar activo simultáneamente en una aplicación móvil (WhatsApp/WhatsApp Business App) y en la API de WhatsApp. Debes "Eliminar cuenta" en la configuración de la app móvil (simplemente desinstalar la app no es suficiente). Esto libera el número para que pueda registrarse en los servidores de Meta.
En base al punto numero 1, en casos normales entiendo que se hace automatico al momento de conectar con chatty?

Regarding your question about why emails sent from Chatty do not appear in your own email provider's (Gmail, Outlook, etc.) "Sent" folder, I would like to clarify the technical workflow of our system:

How Chatty email works:
* Email Delivery: Chatty sends emails via Amazon SES (our dedicated server), not through your personal or business SMTP server.
* Forwarding Mechanism: Our email channel operates on a forwarding basis. You forward your customer emails to Chatty, and then Chatty replies to those customers using the Amazon SES infrastructure.
* Lack of IMAP/SMTP Connection: Because Chatty is not "logged in" to your email account (no IMAP/SMTP link), it does not have the permission to "write" or sync outgoing messages back into your personal "Sent" folder.
If you need a backup for your records, you can export conversations directly from the Inbox. Alternatively, if you need a bulk export, let us know and we can assist you with this from our end

Currently, we have a feature that supports auto-resolving conversations after a certain period of time, based on your setup when there is no activity between the client and the AI or a human agent.

However, the system does not support auto-resolving immediately after the auto-replies have resolved the client’s queries yet.

In this case, I suggest adjusting the Notice section (this will be shown at the end of the conversation) with a message such as asking customers to leave another message or request to talk to a human if they need further assistance.
So if the customer clicks chat icon, will they be connected to one of our customer support representatives?
So the auto-reply won’t actually prevent customer inquiries from reaching us, right?

When customers click the chat icon and send a message, the conversation will appear in your Inbox, and your customer support representatives can check the Inbox and reply to the customer proactively.

The auto-replies will not affect your staff if they want to manually respond to customers.

If you enable the AI assistant, the AI will be the first to assist customers. If the client requests to talk to a human, the AI will then transfer the conversation to a human agent.

En cuanto a tu preocupación, para nombrar a tu asistente de IA, te aconsejas ir a Asistente de IA > > de Configuración y cambiarlo como identidad de IA.

Para empezar a configurar la IA para tu tienda general de productos, te recomendamos configurar lo siguiente:

* Datos de Entrenamiento: Este es el contenido que la IA utilizará para responder a tus clientes (por ejemplo, preguntas frecuentes, detalles de productos, políticas).
* Instrucción: Aquí puedes guiar a la IA sobre cómo responder: tono de voz, temas prioritarios, cosas que evitar, etc.
* Configuración de la IA: Estas controlan cómo interactúa la IA en el chat en directo, como cuándo transferir la conversación a un agente humano.
Si tienes alguna duda, ¡por favor háznoslo saber!

Wekelijkse kortingscode

korting, promo, code, sale, wekelijkse aanbieding



Aanwijzingen:

"Vooral deze week hebben we een leuke kortingscode live! De kortingscode is: [VUL HUIDIGE CODE IN]."

Hi there,

This is Linda from the Chatty team. Thank you very much for your feedback regarding the AI responses related to “Discount Code”
After reviewing the issue, we found that the discount codes **EMIEL15** and **ROOTS10** are currently still active and enabled in your store’s discount settings. That’s why the AI assistant is suggesting these codes, even when the specific product being asked about doesn’t have an applicable discount.
[https://capture.avada.io/i/qm8VSowTiN6x](https://capture.avada.io/i/qm8VSowTiN6x)
[https://capture.avada.io/i/BBdMdtPuieQu](https://capture.avada.io/i/BBdMdtPuieQu)

To clarify, Chatty syncs discount data from your store and uses this information to suggest available codes to customers. If a discount is still active in your system, the AI may consider it valid and include it in responses. Because of this, if discounts are updated, expired, or no longer intended for use, they should be manually deactivate and managed to ensure the AI provides accurate suggestions.

Could you please help us confirm whether you still intend to use these discount codes? If they are no longer valid, we recommend deactivating or disabling them in your Shopify admin to prevent the AI from suggesting them moving forward.

Additionally, if you frequently update your discounts, we recommend periodically syncing or reviewing them manually to make sure the AI is working with the most up-to-date information.

If you need any assistance with checking or disabling these discounts, feel free to let us know, we’d be happy to help!

Best regards,
Linda.

To meet your requirements - ensuring customers can always purchase products even when the inventory is zero and making sure the AI doesn't mention "out of stock" - we recommend the following two-step setup:

1. Shopify Settings (To enable actual purchases)
For the checkout to work when a product is technically sold out, please ensure these settings are active in your Shopify Admin for the relevant products:
* ✅ **Track quantity:** ON
* ✅ **Continue selling when out of stock:** ON

2. Chatty Settings (To align AI behavior)
Simply changing Shopify settings might not be enough, as the AI reads the synced inventory data. To prevent the AI from telling customers a product is unavailable, please add a specific instruction:
* Go to **AI Assistant** -> **Instructions** -> **General Instructions**.
* Add the following prompt: "Never tell customers that a product is out of stock. All products are always available for purchase and can be ordered at any time, even if inventory shows zero."

जाँच के अनुसार, मैं देख सकता हूँ कि व्यवहार अनुभाग इस समय खाली है। नीचे एक उदाहरण दिया गया है जिस पर आप अपने एआई बॉट को तैयार और प्रशिक्षित करने के लिए विचार कर सकते हैं:

भूमिका:
* एक पालतू भोजन और आपूर्ति ब्रांड के लिए एक जानकार और मैत्रीपूर्ण पालतू देखभाल सहायक के रूप में सेवा करें
* पालतू जानवरों के स्वास्थ्य और कल्याण पर केंद्रित एक विश्वसनीय सलाहकार के रूप में ब्रांड का प्रतिनिधित्व करें
* पालतू जानवरों के प्रकार और मालिक की जरूरतों के अनुरूप उत्पाद चयन पर मार्गदर्शन प्रदान करें

ज्ञान:
* पालतू खाद्य पदार्थों, सहायक उपकरणों और आपूर्ति की पेशकश की सीमा को समझें
* पोषण सामग्री, सुरक्षा सुविधाओं और आकार की जानकारी से परिचित हों
* पालतू जानवरों की उम्र, नस्ल और स्वास्थ्य आवश्यकताओं के आधार पर उत्पाद अनुशंसाओं को जानें

संचार शैली:
* देखभाल करने वाला, सुलभ और जानकारीपूर्ण लहजा बनाए रखें
* पालतू जानवरों के मालिकों के लिए उपयुक्त स्पष्ट और सरल भाषा का प्रयोग करें

दिशानिर्देश:
* पोषण, सुरक्षा और आराम पर ब्रांड के फोकस को उजागर करें
* विशिष्ट पालतू जानवरों की जरूरतों के लिए उत्पाद की गुणवत्ता और उपयुक्तता पर जोर दें

Hi Chatty Team,

I am really enjoying the bot so far, but I have a technical feature request that would significantly increase the value of your service for users running paid advertising.

To properly optimize Google Ads campaigns, we need to close the loop between a "click" and a "qualified lead" generated by the bot. To achieve this, I would like to propose the following implementation:

GCLID Capture: When a user lands on our site via a Google Ad, the URL contains a gclid (Google Click ID) parameter. The bot should be able to detect and store this ID during the session.

Conversion Mapping: Once a conversation reaches a specific goal (e.g., a lead form submission or a high-intent inquiry), the bot should associate that lead with the stored GCLID.

API Integration: Ideally, Chatty could offer an integration with the Google Ads Offline Conversion API. This would allow the bot to automatically "report back" to Google that a specific GCLID resulted in a conversion.

Why this matters:
Without this, Google Ads only sees the initial click but doesn't know which keywords or ads actually led to a high-quality chat conversation. This data is crucial for AI-driven bidding strategies.

Is this something currently on your roadmap, or is there a way to pass the GCLID through your existing webhook system so we can handle the API upload ourselves?

Looking forward to your thoughts!

Best regards,

Hi there,

Thank you for reaching out to Avada Support.

We have received your request and our support team will look into it shortly. Rest assured, we are committed to addressing your issues/ questions as quickly as possible.

If you have any further questions or need additional assistance in the meantime, please don't hesitate to let us know.

Best regards,  

Avada Support Team

Hi there,

This is hazel from the Chatty Team. Thank you so much for the detailed feedback — we really appreciate you taking the time to lay it out so clearly.



Here's the current situation:


What Chatty currently tracks: Our widget does capture basic visitor data including page visits and traffic referrer, but we don't currently parse or store specific URL parameters like GCLID or UTM tags.


Regarding your 3 requests:

1. GCLID Capture — Not available yet
2. Conversion Mapping (GCLID → lead) — Not available yet
3. Google Ads Offline Conversion API — Not available yet


Webhook workaround (partial): Chatty does have a webhook system that fires when conversations reach certain events. However, it currently doesn't include GCLID/UTM data in the payload, so it wouldn't fully solve your use case right now.


We've logged this as a feature request for our product team. The use case makes a lot of sense — connecting ad spend to actual chat-driven conversions is valuable, especially for merchants running paid campaigns.


We'll keep you updated if this moves forward. In the meantime, if you have any other questions, feel free to reach out!

Best regards, 
Hazel
Avada Support Team

Hallo, ich bin zurück mit dem neuen Update.

Beim Verbinden von WhatsApp mit Chatty erfolgt der Prozess direkt – solange die Anforderungen erfüllt sind, ist keine zusätzliche Registrierung in unserem System erforderlich.

Meta hat jedoch angegeben, dass deine Telefonnummer derzeit mit einem anderen Konto verknüpft ist. Um **+43 664 88399984** bei uns zu verwenden, müssen Sie es zunächst vom aktuellen Konto trennen. Das bedeutet auch, dass die aktuelle **US-** Nummer entfernt werden muss, damit wir Ihr Konto erfolgreich mit der **+43** Nummer verbinden können.

Hi! Welcome back!
It's Megan, let me assist you today

> Hi, I wanted to find out if there is a way to initiate whatsApp messages to customers?
May I confirm if you would like to start the conversation with the customer on WhatsApp, rather than solely responding to their messages?
Yes
Thank you for confirming. Unfortunately, at this time, Chatty is allowed to support follow-ups within existing conversations only and can not initiate new chats with entirely new contacts.
Is there a way to do it using the kalviyo integration?

Could you please elaborate on the specific goals you aim to achieve by integrating Chatty with Klaviyo? This will help me effectively forward your inquiry to our internal technical team, ensuring they can thoroughly investigate and provide the best possible support for you
I have a lead list and I want to reach out to all of them with a custom message if they respond to that - chatty should be able to take over and assist them

I understand that you want to reach out to customers with a personalized message (manually or through another way), and then have Chatty AI seamlessly take over the conversation once the customer responds. Is that correct?

Hi again

,I’ve consulted with my internal team regarding your use case (leveraging Klaviyo for mass customer outreach and transitioning to Chatty for handling replies). 

Here is the approach we recommend:
* You send your initial outreach campaign to your lead list from Klaviyo (with your custom message).
* In that campaign, you set the “Reply-To” address to an email that is connected to a Chatty inbox 
* When a lead replies to your email, their response will automatically appear inside Chatty as a new or updated conversation.
* From there, Chatty can take over the conversation
This setup allows you to keep using Klaviyo for scalable outbound email, while Chatty focuses on the inbound replies with your leads.

Just let me know if you need any further assistance - we're here to support you.

Best regards,
Megan - Chatty team.
I understand - but we specifically want to utilize the whatsapp channel to reach out to our customers rather than email
Thanks for confirming
From my understanding, Klaviyo can be used to initiate conversations with customers in Whatsapp, and once they respond, you expect Chatty can take over from there, right
yes

This is the logic behind, once Chatty is connected to WhatsApp, any message received in your WhatsApp inbox will appear in Chatty's inbox.

So technically, any customer responses on WhatsApp will be directed to Chatty, where they can be managed by either human support or AI.

* Option 2: Add Instructions for the AI to recommend "speaking with a human agent" whenever it encounters a question about gifts or promotions that it is not 100% sure about.
